verses.latin_vulgate: Væ coronæ superbiæ, ebriis Ephraim,  et flori decidenti, gloriæ exsultationis ejus,  qui erant in vertice vallis pinguissimæ,  errantes a vino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וֹי עֲטֶ֤רֶת גֵּאוּת֙ שִׁכֹּרֵ֣י אֶפְרַ֔יִם וְצִ֥יץ נֹבֵ֖ל צְבִ֣י תִפְאַרְתּ֑וֹ אֲשֶׁ֛ר עַל־רֹ֥אשׁ גֵּֽיא־שְׁמָנִ֖ים הֲל֥וּמֵי יָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: Woe to the crown of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim, whose glorious beauty is a fading flower, which are on the head of the fat valleys of them that are overcome with wine!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce validus et fortis Dominus  sicut impetus grandinis ; turbo confringens,  sicut impetus aquarum multarum inundantium  et emissarum super terram spatiosam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֨ה חָזָ֤ק וְאַמִּץ֙ לַֽאדֹנָ֔י כְּזֶ֥רֶם בָּרָ֖ד שַׂ֣עַר קָ֑טֶב כְּ֠זֶ֠רֶם מַ֣יִם כַּבִּירִ֥ים שֹׁטְפִ֛ים הִנִּ֥יחַ לָאָ֖רֶץ בְּיָֽד׃
verses.king_james: Behold, the Lord hath a mighty and strong one, which as a tempest of hail and a destroying storm, as a flood of mighty waters overflowing, shall cast down to the earth with the hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pedibus conculcabitur  corona superbiæ ebriorum Ephraim.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרַגְלַ֖יִם תֵּרָמַ֑סְנָה עֲטֶ֥רֶת גֵּא֖וּת שִׁכּוֹרֵ֥י אֶפְרָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: The crown of pride, the drunkards of Ephraim, shall be trodden under feet:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit flos decidens gloriæ exsultationis ejus,  qui est super verticem vallis pinguium,  quasi temporaneum ante maturitatem autumni,  quod, cum aspexerit videns,  statim ut manu tenuerit, devorabit illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽהָ֨יְתָ֜ה צִיצַ֤ת נֹבֵל֙ צְבִ֣י תִפְאַרְתּ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֥ר עַל־רֹ֖אשׁ גֵּ֣יא שְׁמָנִ֑ים כְּבִכּוּרָהּ֙ בְּטֶ֣רֶם קַ֔יִץ אֲשֶׁ֨ר יִרְאֶ֤ה הָרֹאֶה֙ אוֹתָ֔הּ בְּעוֹדָ֥הּ בְּכַפּ֖וֹ יִבְלָעֶֽנָּה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And the glorious beauty, which is on the head of the fat valley, shall be a fading flower, and as the hasty fruit before the summer; which when he that looketh upon it seeth, while it is yet in his hand he eateth it up.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa erit Dominus exercituum corona gloriæ,  et sertum exsultationis residuo populi sui ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֗וּא יִֽהְיֶה֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת לַעֲטֶ֣רֶת צְבִ֔י וְלִצְפִירַ֖ת תִּפְאָרָ֑ה לִשְׁאָ֖ר עַמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: In that day shall the Lord of hosts be for a crown of glory, and for a diadem of beauty, unto the residue of his people,

verses.latin_vulgate: et spiritus judicii sedenti super judicium,  et fortitudo revertentibus de bello ad portam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְר֖וּחַ מִשְׁפָּ֑ט לַיּוֹשֵׁב֙ עַל־הַמִּשְׁפָּ֔ט וְלִ֨גְבוּרָ֔ה מְשִׁיבֵ֥י מִלְחָמָ֖ה שָֽׁעְרָה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And for a spirit of judgment to him that sitteth in judgment, and for strength to them that turn the battle to the gate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verum hi quoque præ vino nescierunt, et præ ebrietate erraverunt ;  sacerdos et propheta nescierunt præ ebrietate ;  absorpti sunt a vino, erraverunt in ebrietate,  nescierunt videntem, ignoraverunt judicium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְגַם־אֵ֙לֶּה֙ בַּיַּ֣יִן שָׁג֔וּ וּבַשֵּׁכָ֖ר תָּע֑וּ כֹּהֵ֣ן וְנָבִיא֩ שָׁג֨וּ בַשֵּׁכָ֜ר נִבְלְע֣וּ מִן־הַיַּ֗יִן תָּעוּ֙ מִן־הַשֵּׁכָ֔ר שָׁגוּ֙ בָּרֹאֶ֔ה פָּק֖וּ פְּלִילִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: But they also have erred through wine, and through strong drink are out of the way; the priest and the prophet have erred through strong drink, they are swallowed up of wine, they are out of the way through strong drink; they err in vision, they stumble in judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes enim mensæ repletæ sunt vomitu sordiumque,  ita ut non esset ultra locus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֚י כׇּל־שֻׁלְחָנ֔וֹת מָלְא֖וּ קִ֣יא צֹאָ֑ה בְּלִ֖י מָקֽוֹם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: For all tables are full of vomit and filthiness, so that there is no place clean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quem docebit scientiam ?  et quem intelligere faciet auditum ?  Ablactatos a lacte,  avulsos ab uberibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־מִי֙ יוֹרֶ֣ה דֵעָ֔ה וְאֶת־מִ֖י יָבִ֣ין שְׁמוּעָ֑ה גְּמוּלֵי֙ מֵחָלָ֔ב עַתִּיקֵ֖י מִשָּׁדָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: Whom shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to understand doctrine? them that are weaned from the milk, and drawn from the breasts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia manda, remanda ; manda, remanda ;  exspecta, reexspecta ; exspecta, reexspecta ;  modicum ibi, modicum ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י צַ֤ו לָצָו֙ צַ֣ו לָצָ֔ו קַ֥ו לָקָ֖ו קַ֣ו לָקָ֑ו זְעֵ֥יר שָׁ֖ם זְעֵ֥יר שָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: For precept must be upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little:

verses.latin_vulgate: In loquela enim labii,  et lingua altera  loquetur ad populum istum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֚י בְּלַעֲגֵ֣י שָׂפָ֔ה וּבְלָשׁ֖וֹן אַחֶ֑רֶת יְדַבֵּ֖ר אֶל־הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: For with stammering lips and another tongue will he speak to this people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui dixit : Hæc est requies mea,  reficite lassum ;  et hoc est meum refrigerium :  et noluerunt audire.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר ׀ אָמַ֣ר אֲלֵיהֶ֗ם זֹ֤את הַמְּנוּחָה֙ הָנִ֣יחוּ לֶעָיֵ֔ף וְזֹ֖את הַמַּרְגֵּעָ֑ה וְלֹ֥א אָב֖וּא שְׁמֽוֹעַ׃
verses.king_james: To whom he said, This is the rest wherewith ye may cause the weary to rest; and this is the refreshing: yet they would not hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit eis verbum Domini :  Manda, remanda ; manda, remanda ;  exspecta, reexspecta ; exspecta, reexspecta ;  modicum ibi, modicum ibi ;  ut vadant, et cadant retrorsum,  et conterantur, et illaqueentur, et capiantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֨ה לָהֶ֜ם דְּבַר־יְהֹוָ֗ה צַ֣ו לָצָ֞ו צַ֤ו לָצָו֙ קַ֤ו לָקָו֙ קַ֣ו לָקָ֔ו זְעֵ֥יר שָׁ֖ם זְעֵ֣יר שָׁ֑ם לְמַ֨עַן יֵלְכ֜וּ וְכָשְׁל֤וּ אָחוֹר֙ וְנִשְׁבָּ֔רוּ וְנוֹקְשׁ֖וּ וְנִלְכָּֽדוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But the word of the Lord was unto them precept upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little; that they might go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc audite verbum Domini, viri illusores,  qui dominamini super populum meum, qui est in Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֛ן שִׁמְע֥וּ דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אַנְשֵׁ֣י לָצ֑וֹן מֹֽשְׁלֵי֙ הָעָ֣ם הַזֶּ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֖ר בִּירוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore hear the word of the Lord, ye scornful men, that rule this people which is in Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixistis enim : Percussimus fœdus cum morte,  et cum inferno fecimus pactum :  flagellum inundans cum transierit, non veniet super nos  quia posuimus mendacium spem nostram,  et mendacio protecti sumus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י אֲמַרְתֶּ֗ם כָּרַ֤תְנֽוּ בְרִית֙ אֶת־מָ֔וֶת וְעִם־שְׁא֖וֹל עָשִׂ֣ינוּ חֹזֶ֑ה (שיט) [שׁ֣וֹט] שׁוֹטֵ֤ף כִּי־[יַֽעֲבֹר֙] (עבר) לֹ֣א יְבוֹאֵ֔נוּ כִּ֣י שַׂ֧מְנוּ כָזָ֛ב מַחְסֵ֖נוּ וּבַשֶּׁ֥קֶר נִסְתָּֽרְנוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Because ye have said, We have made a covenant with death, and with hell are we at agreement; when the overflowing scourge shall pass through, it shall not come unto us: for we have made lies our refuge, and under falsehood have we hid ourselves:

verses.latin_vulgate: Idcirco hæc dicit Dominus Deus :  Ecce ego mittam in fundamentis Sion lapidem,  lapidem probatum,  angularem, pretiosum, in fundamento fundatum ;  qui crediderit, non festinet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ אֲדֹנָ֣י יֱהֹוִ֔ה הִנְנִ֛י יִסַּ֥ד בְּצִיּ֖וֹן אָ֑בֶן אֶ֣בֶן בֹּ֜חַן פִּנַּ֤ת יִקְרַת֙ מוּסָ֣ד מוּסָּ֔ד הַֽמַּאֲמִ֖ין לֹ֥א יָחִֽישׁ׃
verses.king_james: Therefore thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I lay in Zion for a foundation a stone, a tried stone, a precious corner stone, a sure foundation: he that believeth shall not make haste.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ponam in pondere judicium,  et justitiam in mensura ;  et subvertet grando spem mendacii,  et protectionem aquæ inundabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׂמְתִּ֤י מִשְׁפָּט֙ לְקָ֔ו וּצְדָקָ֖ה לְמִשְׁקָ֑לֶת וְיָעָ֤ה בָרָד֙ מַחְסֵ֣ה כָזָ֔ב וְסֵ֥תֶר מַ֖יִם יִשְׁטֹֽפוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Judgment also will I lay to the line, and righteousness to the plummet: and the hail shall sweep away the refuge of lies, and the waters shall overflow the hiding place.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et delebitur fœdus vestrum cum morte,  et pactum vestrum cum inferno non stabit :  flagellum inundans cum transierit, eritis ei in conculcationem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכֻפַּ֤ר בְּרִֽיתְכֶם֙ אֶת־מָ֔וֶת וְחָזוּתְכֶ֥ם אֶת־שְׁא֖וֹל לֹ֣א תָק֑וּם שׁ֤וֹט שׁוֹטֵף֙ כִּ֣י יַֽעֲבֹ֔ר וִהְיִ֥יתֶם ל֖וֹ לְמִרְמָֽס׃
verses.king_james: And your covenant with death shall be disannulled, and your agreement with hell shall not stand; when the overflowing scourge shall pass through, then ye shall be trodden down by it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quandocumque pertransierit, tollet vos,  quoniam in mane diluculo pertransibit in die et in nocte ;  et tantummodo sola vexatio intellectum dabit auditui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִדֵּ֤י עׇבְרוֹ֙ יִקַּ֣ח אֶתְכֶ֔ם כִּֽי־בַבֹּ֧קֶר בַּבֹּ֛קֶר יַעֲבֹ֖ר בַּיּ֣וֹם וּבַלָּ֑יְלָה וְהָיָ֥ה רַק־זְוָעָ֖ה הָבִ֥ין שְׁמוּעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: From the time that it goeth forth it shall take you: for morning by morning shall it pass over, by day and by night: and it shall be a vexation only to understand the report.

verses.latin_vulgate: Coangustatum est enim stratum, ita ut alter decidat ;  et pallium breve utrumque operire non potest.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־קָצַ֥ר הַמַּצָּ֖ע מֵהִשְׂתָּרֵ֑עַ וְהַמַּסֵּכָ֥ה צָ֖רָה כְּהִתְכַּנֵּֽס׃
verses.king_james: For the bed is shorter than that a man can stretch himself on it: and the covering narrower than that he can wrap himself in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim in monte divisionum stabit Dominus ;  sicut in valle quæ est in Gabaon irascetur,  ut faciat opus suum, alienum opus ejus :  ut operetur opus suum, peregrinum est opus ejus ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י כְהַר־פְּרָצִים֙ יָק֣וּם יְהֹוָ֔ה כְּעֵ֖מֶק בְּגִבְע֣וֹן יִרְגָּ֑ז לַעֲשׂ֤וֹת מַעֲשֵׂ֙הוּ֙ זָ֣ר מַעֲשֵׂ֔הוּ וְלַֽעֲבֹד֙ עֲבֹ֣דָת֔וֹ נׇכְרִיָּ֖ה עֲבֹדָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For the Lord shall rise up as in mount Perazim, he shall be wroth as in the valley of Gibeon, that he may do his work, his strange work; and bring to pass his act, his strange act.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc nolite illudere,  ne forte constringantur vincula vestra ;  consummationem enim et abbreviationem audivi  a Domino Deo exercituum, super universam terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּה֙ אַל־תִּתְלוֹצָ֔צוּ פֶּֽן־יֶחְזְק֖וּ מֽוֹסְרֵיכֶ֑ם כִּֽי־כָלָ֨ה וְנֶחֱרָצָ֜ה שָׁמַ֗עְתִּי מֵאֵ֨ת אֲדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֛ה צְבָא֖וֹת עַל־כׇּל־הָאָֽרֶץ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Now therefore be ye not mockers, lest your bands be made strong: for I have heard from the Lord God of hosts a consumption, even determined upon the whole earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auribus percipite, et audite vocem meam :  attendite, et audite eloquium meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַאֲזִ֥ינוּ וְשִׁמְע֖וּ קוֹלִ֑י הַקְשִׁ֥יבוּ וְשִׁמְע֖וּ אִמְרָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Give ye ear, and hear my voice; hearken, and hear my speech.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid tota die arabit arans ut serat ?  proscindet et sarriet humum suam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲכֹ֣ל הַיּ֔וֹם יַחֲרֹ֥שׁ הַחֹרֵ֖שׁ לִזְרֹ֑עַ יְפַתַּ֥ח וִֽישַׂדֵּ֖ד אַדְמָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Doth the plowman plow all day to sow? doth he open and break the clods of his ground?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne cum adæquaverit faciem ejus,  seret gith et cyminum sparget ?  et ponet triticum per ordinem, et hordeum,  et milium, et viciam in finibus suis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלוֹא֙ אִם־שִׁוָּ֣ה פָנֶ֔יהָ וְהֵפִ֥יץ קֶ֖צַח וְכַמֹּ֣ן יִזְרֹ֑ק וְשָׂ֨ם חִטָּ֤ה שׂוֹרָה֙ וּשְׂעֹרָ֣ה נִסְמָ֔ן וְכֻסֶּ֖מֶת גְּבֻלָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: When he hath made plain the face thereof, doth he not cast abroad the fitches, and scatter the cummin, and cast in the principal wheat and the appointed barley and the rie in their place?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erudiet illum in judicio ;  Deus suus docebit illum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִסְּר֥וֹ לַמִּשְׁפָּ֖ט אֱלֹהָ֥יו יוֹרֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: For his God doth instruct him to discretion, and doth teach him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim in serris triturabitur gith,  nec rota plaustri super cyminum circuibit ;  sed in virga excutietur gith,  et cyminum in baculo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י לֹ֤א בֶחָרוּץ֙ י֣וּדַשׁ קֶ֔צַח וְאוֹפַ֣ן עֲגָלָ֔ה עַל־כַּמֹּ֖ן יוּסָּ֑ב כִּ֧י בַמַּטֶּ֛ה יֵחָ֥בֶט קֶ֖צַח וְכַמֹּ֥ן בַּשָּֽׁבֶט׃
verses.king_james: For the fitches are not threshed with a threshing instrument, neither is a cart wheel turned about upon the cummin; but the fitches are beaten out with a staff, and the cummin with a rod.

verses.latin_vulgate: Panis autem comminuetur ;  verum non in perpetuum triturans triturabit illum,  neque vexabit eum rota plaustri,  neque ungulis suis comminuet eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֶ֣חֶם יוּדָ֔ק כִּ֛י לֹ֥א לָנֶ֖צַח אָד֣וֹשׁ יְדוּשֶׁ֑נּוּ וְ֠הָמַ֠ם גִּלְגַּ֧ל עֶגְלָת֛וֹ וּפָרָשָׁ֖יו לֹ֥א יְדֻקֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Bread corn is bruised; because he will not ever be threshing it, nor break it with the wheel of his cart, nor bruise it with his horsemen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc a Domino Deo exercituum exivit,  ut mirabile faceret consilium, et magnificaret justitiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־זֹ֕את מֵעִ֛ם יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָא֖וֹת יָצָ֑אָה הִפְלִ֣א עֵצָ֔ה הִגְדִּ֖יל תּוּשִׁיָּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: This also cometh forth from the Lord of hosts, which is wonderful in counsel, and excellent in working.
